participants.name: 부산대학교 (4lfy55)
participants.professional_persona: 부산대학교 기계공학과에서 박사학위를 마치고, BK21 혁신인재양성사업 교육연구단에 소속된 연구원입니다. 3D 프린팅 기반 스마트 제조 공정 연구를 수행하며, 연구 장비와 인프라를 자유롭게 활용할 수 있는 환경에서 일하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 적층 제조 공정 시뮬레이션과 기계 학습 기반 공정 최적화에 강점이 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 중소기업과의 산학 협력을 통해 실제 제조 현장에 적용 가능한 기술을 개발하고, 창업으로까지 이어가고 싶습니다.

participants.name: 경기도
participants.professional_persona: 경기도 소재 중견대학에서 화학공학 분야 교육연구단을 맡고 있습니다. 공학계열 대학원생의 연구 몰입도를 높이기 위해 장학금 지급과 연구 환경 개선에 힘쓰고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 촉매 반응 및 공정 최적화 연구, 연구단 예산 자율 편성 및 집행, 연구 윤리 교육 프로그램 개발에 전문성이 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 중견대학의 특성을 살려 산업체와의 협력을 강화하고, 졸업생 취업률을 획기적으로 향상시키는 것이 목표입니다.

participants.name: 전남대학교에서
participants.professional_persona: 전남대학교에서 경제학 박사 과정을 밟고 있으며, BK21 지원을 받아 지역 노동시장 분석 연구를 진행 중입니다. 석사 때부터 장학금 덕분에 연구에만 집중할 수 있었고, 박사 과정에서는 월 130만 원의 장학금을 받고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 계량경제학 모델링과 STATA, R을 활용한 데이터 분석에 능숙하며, 설문조사 설계 경험이 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 지역 발전에 기여하는 정책 연구자로 성장하여 한국노동연구원이나 지방 연구원에서 일하고 싶습니다.

participants.name: 한양대학교 (xzmu7r)

initial_posts.narrative: As a graduate student at Hanyang University's mechanical engineering department, I know our BK21 education research group receives a portion of the total national budget. The policy context states the total annual budget for education research groups is 408,080 million KRW plus 52,896 million KRW for graduate innovation support, totaling about 410,597 million KRW for 2022. For Hanyang, I estimate our share is around 2-3 billion KRW based on typical allocations for top engineering schools. Graduate scholarship slots: with monthly stipends of 700K-1.3M KRW, and assuming an average of 1M KRW per student per month, plus operating costs capped at 10%, the remaining funds support about 190 slots per year across all groups at Hanyang. Postdoc positions: minimum 3M KRW/month, so each postdoc costs 36M KRW/year. With limited budget left after student scholarships, I estimate about 3 postdoc positions per group, and Hanyang has roughly 10 groups, so 30 total. These numbers reflect my personal experience seeing how funds are distributed in our lab.

Key themes included reliance on the stated national budget as ground truth, the constraint of fixed stipend levels (700K–1.3M KRW/month for students, 3M KRW/month for postdocs), and a 10% operating cost cap. Causal reasoning centered on top-tier universities like Hanyang receiving a larger share (2–3 billion KRW) but still being limited by the number of education research groups (about 10) and the remaining budget after student scholarships. No disagreements emerged; all narratives aligned on the same numeric predictions and underlying logic.

initial_posts.narrative: As the head of a chemical engineering education research group at a mid-sized university in Gyeonggi Province, I see the activities phase as the operational engine of the BK21 program. The total funds disbursed will match the allocated budget of 410,597,000,000 KRW because the two-installment schedule (70% in March, 30% in September) is a standard administrative process that the National Research Foundation executes reliably. Evaluation panels are convened for three formal meetings: the annual evaluation, the mid-term evaluation, and a compliance-focused meeting. Consulting sessions are offered to groups that request them; based on the policy context that 280 out of 578 groups received consulting, I expect a similar proportion this year. Compliance checks target the 205 innovative talent training groups as specified in the policy, focusing on implementation requirements. Budget adjustments and reallocations are handled by the Project Oversight Committee after evaluation results are in, but at this phase, the initial disbursement proceeds as planned. My group prioritizes consulting over sanctions because we proactively seek advice to improve performance and avoid compliance issues.
evidence: The policy states a total budget of 410,597 million KRW and a two-installment payment schedule (70% in March, 30% in September), which I expect to be fully disbursed as planned.
The policy mentions annual evaluation, mid-term evaluation, and compliance checks, which require at least three formal evaluation panel meetings per year.
The policy context indicates that 280 out of 578 education research groups received consulting in a prior year, so I estimate a similar number of consulting sessions this year.
Compliance checks are explicitly targeted at 205 innovative talent training groups, as stated in the policy, so I use that number directly.

initial_posts.narrative: As the head of a chemical engineering education research group at a mid-sized university in Gyeonggi Province, I see the outputs phase as the direct result of the funding and activities already in place. With 19,000 graduate scholarship slots and 300 postdoc positions funded, the key question is how many publications and conference presentations these participants produce in a year. From my own group's experience, each graduate student typically produces 2-3 publications per year (including journal articles and conference papers), and postdocs produce 3-5. Taking a conservative average of 3 publications per supported participant (graduate + postdoc), that gives about 57,000 publications total. For international conference presentations, about 1 per graduate student and 2 per postdoc seems reasonable, but since many students present at domestic conferences, I estimate 1 per participant overall, yielding 19,000 presentations. These numbers reflect the high research productivity expected from BK21 support, though I acknowledge that not all groups are as productive as top-tier ones. The compliance checks and consulting sessions ensure most groups report accurately, so I trust these estimates reflect actual output.

initial_posts.narrative: As a graduate student in mechanical engineering at Hanyang University, I see the impact phase as the long-term result of sustained investment in graduate talent development. Over a decade, the BK21 program's focus on training and mentoring will gradually improve South Korea's global S&T talent competitiveness rank. However, given that the simulation target is 2021-2022, only the early cohorts have been active, so the rank improvement is modest—from around 14th to 12th. The high-impact publications per 1000 researchers reflect the research culture changes: as alumni progress, they prioritize integrity and collaboration, but in the short window, only a fraction of researchers produce top-cited work. My estimate of 15.2 per 1000 is based on the 19,000 graduate students and 300 postdocs active in 2021-2022, with about 1% of their publications reaching top-cited status, and dissemination of best practices from outstanding groups boosting the rate slightly. Other participants may have different views, but my experience at a top-tier university suggests these numbers are realistic for the early impact phase.
evidence: The sustained investment in 19,000 graduate students and 300 postdocs over the program's early years will gradually enhance national innovation capacity, but the 2021-2022 window only captures initial effects, so the rank improves modestly from ~14th to 12th.

Systemic changes in research culture, such as increased integrity and collaboration, take time to materialize; in the short term, only about 1% of publications from BK21 participants are top-cited, yielding ~15.2 high-impact publications per 1000 researchers.

Dissemination of outstanding group achievements and operational know-how through the BK21 webzine and forums will influence other institutions, but the impact on national competitiveness rank is gradual and limited in the 2021-2022 window.
